analysis: v3 added a worked example to anchor fix pattern format, but output regressed severely. All code snippets are placeholder comments ('// ... 70 lines of complex logic') rather than real quoted file content. No fix patterns appear despite the example. The model is not reading actual source files — it's hallucinating findings based on function names and file sizes alone. Rotation plan at the end suggests re-scanning the same 5 files, opposite of the instruction. Zero High/Critical findings despite clearly complex codebase with large files (15K lines in consolidate.ts).
hypothesis: v4: Add an explicit ZERO-TOLERANCE rule on placeholder comments in code snippets. Findings that contain '// ...' or paraphrased code must be discarded. Force the model to either quote exact lines or skip the finding entirely.
